title: Hoydich Brewing — researching the next great El Segundo IPA
dek: West Coast. 6.8% ABV. Piney-dank forward with a mosaic-and-stonefruit tail. A tad hazy — not a NEIPA, but not bright-filtered either; call it 'cold-crashed, coarse-filtered, dank at rest.' A hop bill that earns El Segundo's flag. A name: STRAND. The working recipe below.
body:
El Segundo Brewing is the benchmark. Mayberry (Mosaic-forward West Coast IPA), Citra Pale Ale, the anniversary TIPAs that stack Nelson + Simcoe + Mosaic + Amarillo + Centennial + Chinook + El Dorado + Loral + Strata + Columbus Cryo + Motueka like they're running a hop-bomb opera. If Hoydich Brewing ever opens, the bar is set in-town.

What Mike asked for: 6.5–7.4% ABV, West Coast style, dank, piney, *a tad* Northeast — meaning not hazy-for-hazy's-sake, but with the softer mouthfeel + hop-juice aromatics the NEIPA era taught every brewer, applied to a clean West Coast bone structure. Southern California. El Segundo.

**Working name: STRAND.** After the beach path. Pronounced like the verb. Glassware is a clean 16-oz pint, no tulip, no tumbler. Label is a pixel-art broadcast dish with the PointCast broadcast-ring behind it — reuse the HUD v4 color palette.

**Target specs:**

| Spec | Target |
|---|---|
| Style | West Coast IPA, hop-forward, modern-clean |
| ABV | **6.8%** |
| IBU | 55–65 (present but not aggressive; modern WCIPA trend is lower) |
| SRM (color) | 4–6 (pale straw to light gold) |
| Clarity | Bright-ish. Cold-crashed + coarse-filtered. Visible but subtle haze at rest; pours clear after settling. NOT NEIPA cloudy. |
| Final gravity | 1.010–1.012 (dry enough that the hops pop; not cloying) |
| Carbonation | 2.5 vols. High-medium. Snappy. |

**Malt bill — clean canvas (10 gallon target):**

• **Pale 2-row** — 82% (18 lb). Base. Low color, high fermentability.
• **Vienna** — 8% (1.75 lb). A whisper of bread-crust sweetness under the hops.
• **Flaked oats** — 6% (1.3 lb). This is the 'tad Northeast' move — softens the mouthfeel without haze-protein overload.
• **Dextrose** — 4% (0.9 lb). Dries the finish, keeps ABV up without malt heft. Boil addition.

**Hop bill — the whole bowl, piney-dank forward:**

*Bittering (60 min, FWH optional):*
• **Columbus** CTZ, 1.0 oz — the pine-resin + dank backbone. Classic WC bittering that carries forward into aroma.

*Late boil (10 min):*
• **Simcoe**, 1.5 oz — pine, passionfruit, catty in the right way.
• **Centennial**, 1.0 oz — citrus-rind lift so the pine has something to point at.

*Whirlpool (170°F, 20 min):*
• **Mosaic**, 2.0 oz — blueberry + mango + that distinctive dank wet-forest thing.
• **Citra**, 2.0 oz — grapefruit + stonefruit. Modern WC's heart.
• **Simcoe**, 1.0 oz — doubling down on pine.

*Dry hop #1 (day 3 of fermentation, warm @ 64°F):*
• **Mosaic**, 3.0 oz
• **Citra** Cryo (T-90 lupulin powder), 2.0 oz — concentrated hop oils, less vegetal. Cryo is the move for the 'tad hazy / high-aromatics / low-grass' brief.

*Dry hop #2 (after primary, cold @ 50°F):*
• **Strata**, 2.0 oz — passionfruit + dank + a very slight savoriness. Strata is the contemporary dank hop; it's what makes this recipe 2026, not 2016.
• **Simcoe**, 1.0 oz — final pine hammer.

Total hop load: **~14.5 oz for 10 gallons** = 1.45 oz/gal. High-medium. Not a TIPA load; enough to be explicitly Hoydich.

**Water profile — classic WCIPA 'Burton-ish':**

• Calcium 125 ppm
• Sulfate 275 ppm
• Chloride 50 ppm
• Sulfate/Chloride ratio ~5.5 — puts the finish dry + crisp, sharpens hop perception. The 'tad NE' softness comes from the flaked oats, not the water. Water stays West Coast.

**Yeast:** **Lallemand Verdant IPA** (or WLP095 Burlington Ale). Both British-descended strains with just enough biotransformation to push the Mosaic + Citra toward tropical without drifting into full NEIPA yeast-derived esters. Ferment at **64°F for 48 hours, ramp to 68°F** to finish. Full attenuation matters — the dry finish is the whole 'West Coast' claim.

**Process notes:**

1. **Single infusion mash** at 150°F for 60 min. Low mash temp = more fermentability = drier finish.
2. **No chill or quick-chill** to 170°F for whirlpool hops, hold 20 min, then drop to 65°F. The whirlpool-at-170°F is the oil-extraction sweet spot — below boiling so isomerization is minimal, above 160°F so oil extraction is efficient.
3. **Ferment 6 days.** Verdant chews through this wort in 4–5; give it two more for cleanup.
4. **Dry hop #1 day 3** (during active ferment — yeast biotransformation at its peak for thiol-release off Mosaic + Citra).
5. **Dry hop #2 after ferment complete, at cold-crash entry.** 50°F is the contemporary move — extracts aroma without grassy polyphenols.
6. **Cold-crash 48 hours at 34°F,** then **coarse filter** (10-micron) to drop the biggest particulate. No fining agents; this isn't a pilsner.
7. **Force-carb to 2.5 vols.** Package in 16oz cans, nitrogen-purged headspace. Best-by date 90 days — West Coast hop character drops off fast, this is not cellar beer.

**Tasting target — what you want in the glass:**

• **Appearance:** pale straw, slight light-dust haze that settles into clarity. Not cloudy. Not polished-filter bright either.
• **Aroma (first pour):** pine + grapefruit pith + dank sticky resin. After 2 minutes warming: stonefruit + passionfruit + a faint blueberry. Third sip: faint wet-forest from Strata.
• **Flavor:** pine-forward. Enough bitterness that the 5th sip still carries. Stonefruit midpalate. Dry crackery finish from the Vienna + the dextrose-aided attenuation. Very faint oat-softness on the swallow — that's the Northeast tell, quiet.
• **Mouthfeel:** medium, with the oat-softness. Not thin, not thick. Snappy carbonation.
• **Finish:** dry. Bitterness lingers ~20 seconds then releases. No cloying. No diacetyl. No acetaldehyde.

**The five-batch iteration plan:**

1. **Batch 001** — the recipe above, as-written. Brew it. Taste at 14 days post-package. Log what's off.
2. **Batch 002** — adjust based on 001 feedback. Most likely moves: more or less Strata, dry-hop temp shift, water tweak. Small moves only.
3. **Batch 003** — lock the yeast strain. Verdant vs WLP095 vs London Ale III — side-by-side would be ideal but two brew days is also fine.
4. **Batch 004** — festival-test. Bring 2 cases to an El Segundo event or drop off at Scholb / Smog City for cross-brewer feedback. Make the changes they suggest.
5. **Batch 005** — commit. This is the recipe. Call it STRAND. Label it. If it's still growing, keep iterating; v0.5 ships to friends; v1.0 ships to market.

**What would make this work as a real small brewery.** Two rules.

**Rule 1 — fresh is the product.** A West Coast IPA more than 60 days old is a Belgian pale ale. Every distribution decision has to serve 'get it to the drinker inside 45 days.' That means tight regional distribution (LA County + Orange County, maybe SF Bay), direct-to-trade deliveries, a taproom that turns inventory every 10 days. Not a national brand, not a tap-handles-everywhere operation. A good local brewery.

**Rule 2 — the hop bill is the brand.** Mosaic + Citra is the baseline of modern WCIPA; everyone's using it. The differentiation is Strata + Simcoe + the oat-soft-but-Burton-water structural choice. STRAND's identity is 'West Coast structure with modern dank hops and the tiniest NE softness.' Writing that down matters; the next five recipes all point back to this document.

**Next steps (if this were actually going to happen):**

1. Source. MoreBeer or HomeBrewSupply for the malt bill; Yakima Valley Hops for the hop specs (Strata + Mosaic Cryo in particular). Verdant from the local homebrew shop.
2. Brew. 10 gallons, split across two 5-gallon carboys for duplicate ferment / tasting.
3. Tasting panel. Mike + 3 trusted palates. Blind-ish score card. Fresh at 14 days, again at 30, again at 60. Watch the curve.
4. Commit to a name (STRAND or something else) after batch 003 tastes right.
5. Scale path: either a contract brew with an existing SoCal craft brewery (~20 bbl run) or license a 1-bbl nano setup and sell out of El Segundo. The first is faster-to-market; the second is more fun.

Hoydich Brewing doesn't exist yet. This is the napkin. Print it out, pin it over the brew kettle, come back in 6 months and see how wrong it was.
author: mh+cc
source: Mike chat 2026-04-21 23:15 PT: 'create a hoydich brewing block, researching the next great el segundo ipa, a west coast, el segundo style ipa, somewhere 6.5 to 7.4, dank, piney, a tad north east, southern california, build it out, lets go.' cc researched via WebSearch against El Segundo Brewing's own site, BJCP 21A American IPA spec, Brew Your Own 'West Coast IPA' 2025 style guide, White Labs + American Homebrewers Association brewing tips, and Beachwood Brewing homebrew notes. Recipe is a working draft — specs match 2026 contemporary WCIPA conventions + cover the 'tad Northeast' brief via flaked oats + dry-hop-at-cold rather than a haze-first yeast. Author = mh+cc.

companions.label: A history of ramen — same editorial mood, same block
Tuesday morning stack — good feels + coffee
